# Spherical density estimation

Spherical density estimation

## Usage

``` r
# S3 method for class 'spherical'
density(x, ...)

density_calc(
  x,
  FUN = exponential_kamb,
  n = 128L,
  sigma = 3,
  weights = NULL,
  upper.hem = FALSE,
  r = 1
)
```

## Arguments

- x:

  object of class `"Vec3"`, `"Line"`, `"Ray"`, or `"Plane"`, where the
  rows are the observations and the columns are the coordinates.

- ...:

  arguments passed to `density_calc()`

- FUN:

  density estimation function if `kamb=TRUE`; one of
  [`exponential_kamb()`](https://tobiste.github.io/structr/reference/density-funs.md)
  (the default),
  [kamb_count](https://tobiste.github.io/structr/reference/density-funs.md),
  and
  [`schmidt_count()`](https://tobiste.github.io/structr/reference/density-funs.md).

- n:

  integer. Grid size. `128` by default.

- sigma:

  numeric. Radius for Kamb circle used for counting. 3 by default.

- weights:

  (optional) numeric vector of length of `nrow(x)`. The relative weight
  to be applied to each input measurement. The array will be normalized
  to sum to 1, so absolute value of the `weights` do not affect the
  result. Defaults to `NULL`

- upper.hem:

  logical. Whether the projection is shown for upper hemisphere (`TRUE`)
  or lower hemisphere (`FALSE`, the default).

- r:

  numeric. radius of stereonet circle

## Examples

``` r
set.seed(20250411)
test <- rfb(100, mu = Line(120, 10), k = 5, A = diag(c(-1, 0, 1)))
density(x = test, n = 100, sigma = 3, weights = runif(100))
```
